statement: CoreWeave holds a $21 billion multi-year purchase commitment from Meta running through 2032, even as Meta's new 'Meta Compute' unit emerges as a potential competitor.
why: A $21B locked commitment underpins CoreWeave's forward revenue and signals sustained hyperscaler GPU-capacity demand, while Meta Compute is a competitive risk to future neocloud share.

impact.mechanism: A large multi-year purchase commitment is forward GPU-capacity demand; a new Meta reseller of excess capacity is a competitive supply threat to neoclouds.

evidence.excerpt: despite Meta's own $21B multi-year commitment to CoreWeave through 2032.

confidence.basis: Single secondary source (Motley Fool); no primary filing.
